title: SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES - 211info
text: ACCESS SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a short-term housing intervention program with assistance through case management. Support may include move-in costs, rental assistance, eviction prevention, benefits support, employment and education services, and legal aid around housing barriers. Must be willing to work with a caseworker. May require clients to be alcohol-and drug-free. Assistance depends on the availability of funding and eligibility. 3630 Aviation Way Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: -Veterans and veteran families who are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness -Veteran must be present in household -Any discharge other than dishonorable Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (458) 488-1340 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Intake (458) 488-1340 Coordinator Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: -Call to discuss eligibility and options. Leave a brief message if needed, and a caseworker will return the call. -Depending on the results of the eligibility screening, an appointment may be scheduled -An intake appointment does not guarantee assistance FEES: None DOCUMENTS: DD214, photo ID, Social Security card LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Coos, Curry, Douglas, Jackson, and Josephine Counties Services: Case/Care Management * Homeless Veterans Case/Care Management * Veterans Homeless Permanent Supportive Housing * Veterans Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Veterans Rent Payment Assistance * Homeless Veterans Rent Payment Assistance * Veterans Rental Deposit Assistance * Homeless Veterans Rental Deposit Assistance * Veterans Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY DISASTER SERVICES - CENTER FOR COMMUNITY RESILIENCE FOOD - FOOD PANTRIES FOOD - SENIOR FOOD PANTRIES HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - DURABLE MEDICAL EQUIPMENT PROGRAM HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - OVER THE TOP WIG PROGRAM HOUSING/SHELTER - AFFORDABLE HOUSING HOUSING/SHELTER - HOMEOWNERSHIP CENTER HOUSING/SHELTER - HOUSING RETENTION PROGRAM HOUSING/SHELTER - JACKSON COUNTY CONTINUUM OF CARE SCREENINGS HOUSING/SHELTER - RENT DEPOSIT ASSISTANCE HOUSING/SHELTER - WEATHERIZATION INFORMATION SERVICES - OUTREACH TO SENIORS AND PEOPLE WITH DISABILITIES UTILITIES - ENERGY ASSISTANCE PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. ACCESS
